When: Every Wednesday morning 10:00-10:50 (walk-in from 9:30 for coffee, games, and a chat)
Where: Broedplaats SUP, Surinameplein 33, Amsterdam
For whom: Parents (m/x/f) with child(ren) aged 0-5 years.

What to bring: Yourself and your little one(s)

We advise wearing comfortable clothing.

Follow @movenmeet on Instagram for the latest updates.

title: Parent & Child Movement Theater
description: These unique parent & child movement theatre sessions offer a fun and creative way for toddlers and preschoolers (2-4 years old) and their parents to spend time together. Each week features an imaginative journey exploring a different theme through dance, mime, songs, costumes, and decor, all conducted in English. The sessions foster body awareness, English vocabulary expansion, and social interaction in a welcoming and inclusive environment. Amsterdam Kids Drama & Dance is committed to being LGBT+ family friendly and celebrates all cultural backgrounds. While the studio is not currently wheelchair accessible, they are working on solutions. Sessions are regular, occurring weekly on Wednesdays and Saturdays.
newsletter_description: Join Amsterdam Kids Drama & Dance for fun and creative Parent & Child Movement Theatre Sessions, designed for toddlers and preschoolers aged 2-4 years. These weekly English-language classes use dance, mime, and songs to explore imaginative themes, fostering creativity and interaction in an inclusive, family-friendly atmosphere. Sign up for a block of sessions to join this engaging activity.

title: Reopening of the Vondelpark Klauterpad Playground
description:
On Wednesday, March 25, we will open the renovated play structure at the Klauterpad in Vondelpark. The playground has been out of use for some time, but the play structure has since been refurbished. The greenery in the playground has also been improved.

The renovated Klauterpad will be officially opened with a festive ceremony, attended by the children's mayor and officials from the South district, among others.

From March 25, children (and adults) can use the playground equipment again.
newsletter_description: The wooden Klauterpad playground in Vondelpark is reopening this week! There will be an official opening with a festive ceremony, attended by the children's mayor and city officials. Everyone can play after Wednesday in the new, renovated playground.

title: Cherry Blossom Food Fest & International Sushi Festival
description: Experience the annual Cherry Blossom Food Fest and International Sushi Festival hosted by Japan Light Festival. Held at Bloesempark Amstelveen within the Amsterdamse Bos, this event celebrates the cherry blossom season with a diverse array of food, including an International Sushi Festival, and cultural highlights from Japan and Korea.
newsletter_description: The Japan Light Festival invites families to the Cherry Blossom Food Fest & International Sushi Festival at Bloesempark Amstelveen. Enjoy delicious food and celebrate the cherry blossom season with Japanese and Korean cultural highlights.

title: Robbeburg x Muzaza Musical Playgroup
description: Robbeburg International Playgroup is partnering with Muzaza and its founder, Maya Felixbrodt, for a special morning of music, rhythm, and play. Little ones are invited to explore the magic of music together through singing, movement, and joyful activities. Space is limited, and tickets go on sale Friday, March 7.
newsletter_description: Robbeburg International Playgroup is hosting a special musical playgroup session with Muzaza founder Maya Felixbrodt, offering a joyful morning of music, rhythm, singing, and movement for little ones. This ticketed event, designed for children 0-4 years old, promises a magical exploration of music together. Tickets go on sale March 7.

title: Pregnant Parents Lunch & Meet-up at Papote
description: Hello Doula invites pregnant parents (in their 1st, 2nd, or 3rd trimester) to a lovely lunch at Papote. This event is a perfect opportunity to meet and connect with future mama friends. Your ticket includes a welcome drink and light snacks.
newsletter_description: Join Hello Doula for a lovely lunch at Papote, designed for pregnant parents in their 1st, 2nd or 3rd trimester. It's a perfect opportunity to meet future mama friends and connect. Your ticket includes a welcome drink and light snacks.

title: Japanese Family MATSURI Festival
description: Join Popup collective amsterdam for a Japanese Family MATSURI Festival in Amsterdam Noord! This event for all ages will feature a variety of Japanese activities, including martial arts (like jiu-jitsu), creative workshops such as origami, animation, illustration, and haiku writing. Enjoy Japanese snacks and onigiri, which will be available for purchase, and groove to a DJ set playing Japanese music. The festival takes place at Bethelkerk and promises a vibrant cultural experience for the whole family.
newsletter_description: Popup collective amsterdam invites families to a vibrant Japanese Family MATSURI Festival in Amsterdam Noord, featuring a blend of Japanese sports, creative workshops like origami and animation, and a DJ with Japanese music. Enjoy authentic Japanese snacks and onigiri available for purchase. This engaging cultural event is open to all ages, promising fun for both children and adults.

title: Easter Parade
description: Join Gelderlandplein for their festive Easter Parade! This event includes an exciting Easter egg hunt and various other Easter-themed activities, perfect for families.
newsletter_description: Gelderlandplein invites families to their festive Easter Parade, featuring an exciting Easter egg hunt and other fun activities. This event is perfect for all ages, offering a wonderful way to celebrate the holiday.

title: Opening day of the KiMu Children's Art Museum
description: KiMu, the Children's Art Museum, empowers children in their own creative process through play, experimentation, research, imagination, expression, and autonomy. It is a place where ideas grow, children explore their boundaries, and connect with themselves, each other, and the world. KiMu also serves as a meeting place for parents, educators, and professionals to gain inspiration and practical tools in pedagogy and art education. Visitors can enjoy inspiring exhibitions featuring works by children and contemporary artists, participate in workshops in richly equipped ateliers, explore phenomena of light, and find materials and tools for creative activities at home or in the classroom.
newsletter_description: KiMu, the Children's Art Museum in Amsterdam, is a vibrant space where children lead their own creative journey through play, experimentation, and art. It offers inspiring exhibitions, workshops, and resources for children of all ages, fostering imagination and autonomy. KiMu also serves as a valuable meeting point for parents and educators seeking inspiration in pedagogy and art education.

title: Decorate Your Easter Egg
description: Join Papote for a festive Easter celebration where participants can decorate Easter eggs and enjoy an exciting Easter egg hunt. This event offers multiple 1.5-hour time slots to accommodate families throughout the day. Perfect for creative fun and a memorable experience.
newsletter_description: Papote invites families of all ages to a special 'Decorate Your Easter Egg' event. Participants can look forward to decorating their own Easter eggs and joining a fun egg hunt. This ticketed event offers multiple 1.5-hour time slots on April 1st, 2026, ensuring a festive and memorable experience for everyone.

title: KIEM Easter Bingo for Children
description: Join KIEM for a special Easter Bingo event designed for children! Volunteer Loes has sourced wonderful prizes made from sustainable materials, and Silvester will be present to announce the numbers and ensure a lively atmosphere. The Bingo event specifically runs from 14:00 to 15:00, although the Kindergroencafe KIEM Lokaal is open from 10:00. Participation is free, but advance registration is mandatory and can be done by emailing info@wachterliedpaviljoen.nl.
newsletter_description: KIEM invites children up to 10 years old to a special, free Easter Bingo event. Join them at KIEM Lokaal in Bos en Lommer for a fun afternoon filled with sustainable prizes and lively number calling. Registration is required to secure your spot.

title: Donate to A Different Story
description: A Different Story announces their ambitious plans for 2026, aiming to expand their reach by 200% to serve over 100 families. Their goals include offering more tailored support to parents, providing increased activities for vulnerable young people, and hosting more community events and activities. They are currently raising core funding to achieve these objectives and invite the community to join them in building a different future.
newsletter_description: A Different Story is a local nonprofit that supports families with children that have special needs. They are currently fundraising to double their reach of their core programs: to serve over 100 families with support groups and special events. It's a cause close to my heart, and I invite you to donate if you're able ❤️

title: Kids Easter Egg Hunt
description:
Join District Church for a fun-filled Kids Easter Egg Hunt with prizes, bounce houses, treats, and lots of fun!
11:30 (ages 1–3)
newsletter_description: Join District Church for a fun-filled Kids Easter Egg Hunt, featuring prizes, bounce houses, and treats! This event is perfect for families with young children, promising lots of fun. Registration is required via Eventbrite.
location: 151-hs Van Ostadestraat, 1073 TK Amsterdam, The Netherlands

title: Easter Egg Hunt and Crafts at Willet-Holthuysen
description: Come to Huis Willet-Holthuysen for a special Easter activity over the Easter weekend (April 4, 5 and 6, 2026). Children can enjoy an exciting Easter egg hunt in the historic garden, with many hiding spots for a challenging search. Indoors, there will be guided craft sessions to create beautiful Easter decorations or color coloring pages under supervision. To guarantee a spot, it is recommended to reserve a time slot online. If time slots are sold out, visitors can still try to come on the day itself for entry if space allows. The activities take place daily from 10:00 AM to 3:00 PM.
newsletter_description: Join the Amsterdam Museum at Huis Willet-Holthuysen for a delightful Easter weekend filled with family fun! Children can participate in an exciting Easter egg hunt in the historic garden and get creative with Easter crafts and coloring activities indoors. This family-friendly event, hosted by the Amsterdam Museum, runs from April 4-6, 2026.

title: Paasstraat Easter Event
description: The Paasstraat Easter Event is a festive celebration held on Maasstraat in Amsterdam. It features an Easter Scavenger Hunt available from March 31 to April 4, 2026. A special highlight is the 'Schmink & Glittertantes' (Face Painting & Glitter Aunts) activity, which takes place on Saturday, April 4, 2026, from 11:00 to 15:00. Participants have the chance to win prizes. Additional information can be found on flyers distributed in local shops.
newsletter_description: Join the Paasstraat Easter Event for a delightful celebration on Maasstraat! Enjoy an Easter Scavenger Hunt from March 31 to April 4, and on Saturday, April 4, children can get creative with face painting and glitter. This event, organized by local businesses, is perfect for babies, toddlers, and young children (0-8 years), offering fun activities and a chance to win prizes.

title: Easter Weekend
description: Celebrate Easter Weekend at Breman Brasserie. Enjoy three days of special brunch from 12-4pm with an à la carte menu and a chicken roast for two to share. Dinner from 6-10pm will feature Breman's selects with an Easter twist. A candy cart will be available all weekend, and on Monday from 1-3pm, a few fluffy Alpaca friends will be stopping by on the terrace.
newsletter_description: Join The Hoxton's Breman Brasserie for an enchanting Easter Weekend filled with delicious brunch and dinner, a delightful candy cart, and special visits from fluffy Alpaca friends on the terrace. This family-friendly event offers a festive atmosphere for all ages.

title: Easter Egg Hunt Event in het Amsterdamse Bos
description: Join Vourla Event Planning for an unforgettable Easter Egg Hunt Event at the beautiful Amsterdamse Bos. The day will be filled with excitement, laughter, and plenty of sweet surprises. The Easter Egg Hunt is designed for children of all ages, with special areas for different age groups to ensure everyone has a fantastic time.
newsletter_description: Join Vourla Event Planning for an exciting Easter Egg Hunt at the beautiful Amsterdamse Bos! This event is designed for children of all ages with special areas for different age groups, promising a day filled with laughter and sweet surprises. Tickets are required for participation.
location: Nieuwe Meerlaan 1, 1182 DB Amstelveen, The Netherlands

title: Easter Brunch & Scavenger Hunt 2026
description: Celebrate Easter at De Ceuvel with their annual Easter Brunch and Speurtocht (scavenger hunt). The chef will prepare a delicious brunch buffet featuring fresh, local, and sustainable products, including homemade bread, pastries, various spreads, salads, and warm dishes, catering to all visitors. Children can enjoy a fun scavenger hunt across the site, searching for hidden Easter eggs to earn a tasty surprise. This promises to be an unforgettable day for the whole family! Reservations are required.
newsletter_description: De Ceuvel invites families to their annual Easter Brunch and Scavenger Hunt on Sunday, April 5, 2026. Enjoy a delicious buffet with fresh, local, and sustainable products, and let the children participate in a fun scavenger hunt for hidden Easter eggs. Reservations are required for this delightful family event, open to all ages.
location: Korte Papaverweg 4, 1032 KB Amsterdam, The Netherlands

title: Easter Egg Hunt at Terrasmus
description: Join Terrasmus for an exciting Easter egg hunt in the park! All little ones are invited to search for eggs. The event starts promptly at 10:00 AM on Sunday, April 5th and Monday, April 6th. Terrasmus is looking forward to seeing everyone!
newsletter_description: Terrasmus invites all families to their annual Easter egg hunt in the park! This free event is perfect for young children of all ages to search for hidden eggs. The hunt kicks off promptly at 10:00 AM on both Sunday, April 5th and Monday, April 6th.

title: Papote Easter Egg Hunt
description:
Papote Easter Egg Hunt | Monday 6th of April
Included in the ticket is: a basket, play area entrance and easter pancakes to share as a family 

10 am to 12 pm

Kick off at 10:15 am
newsletter_description: Papote is doing a special opening on Monday for an Easter Egg Hunt! Included in the ticket is: a basket, play area entrance and easter pancakes to share as a family. Kick off at 10:15am.

title: 7 Tips to get your baby sleeping through the night with Workshops
description: Learn 7 gentle, practical tips to help your baby sleep better and create calmer nights for the whole family. Session led by a Sleep Coach Yolanda (@Game of snores). We ask for a symbolic €1 to reserve your spot.
newsletter_description: Learn 7 gentle, practical tips to help your baby sleep better and create calmer nights for the whole family. Session led by a Sleep Coach Yolanda (@Game of snores). We ask for a symbolic €1 to reserve your spot.

title: Navigating Unexplained Infertility: A Holistic Path
description: An intimate workshop, 'A Holistic Path,' combines functional nutrition and nervous system science for women navigating infertility. Led by Vivienne (Florish) and Sonia (Eat Love Thrive Wellness), this small group workshop (maximum 8 participants) explores overlooked aspects of fertility, including gut health, the microbiome, the immune system, minerals, and the nervous system. The session includes an educational presentation on unexplained infertility through a holistic lens, a body awareness exercise, and journaling, aiming to provide a clearer understanding and sense of empowerment for participants. It's designed to complement, not replace, medical advice or fertility treatment.
newsletter_description: Join 'A Holistic Path,' an intimate online workshop hosted by Florish and Eat Love Thrive Wellness. This paid workshop (tickets from €49) is designed for adult women navigating infertility, exploring functional nutrition and nervous system science to offer a deeper understanding of the body's role in fertility challenges.

title: Mom Happy Hour
description: Join parentally.nl for a 'Mom Happy Hour' hosted by Claudia Carter, a Social Emotional Development Specialist and Special Needs Intervention Consultant. This event offers mothers a chance to connect, share experiences, and unwind in Amsterdam's De Pijp neighborhood.
newsletter_description: Join parentally.nl for a 'Mom Happy Hour' hosted by Claudia Carter, a Social Emotional Development Specialist and Special Needs Intervention Consultant. This event provides a fantastic opportunity for mothers to connect, share experiences, and relax in the vibrant De Pijp neighborhood of Amsterdam. Designed for parents, it's a perfect evening to unwind and network.

title: The 3 conversations that are quietly breaking your expat family...and how to repair them
description: This free, inclusive 60-minute live masterclass with Angela Vitiello aims to help expat parents repair critical conversations within their families that may have been lost amidst life abroad. It addresses feelings of loneliness, provides exact language for difficult conversations with partners, and offers a framework for navigating children's identity questions. Participants will learn practical tools to reconnect with themselves, their partners, and their children. A recording will be sent to all who register.
newsletter_description: Join Angela Vitiello for a free online masterclass by Expat Parenting Collective, designed specifically for expat parents. This 60-minute session will help you repair the crucial conversations within your family, addressing common challenges like loneliness and your child's identity questions. Learn practical tools and language to foster deeper connection with yourself, your partner, and your kids.

title: The ABCs of Raising Multilingual Children Workshop
description: A workshop hosted by Growing Up Multilingual at Monti's World, focused on the science, strategies, and soul of raising multilingual children with confidence. It offers a supportive space for families living between languages, cultures, and countries to connect, chat, laugh, and discuss real experiences in raising multilingual kids.
newsletter_description: Join Growing Up Multilingual at Monti's World for 'The ABCs of Raising Multilingual Children' workshop. This event offers science, strategies, and insights for parents raising multilingual children with confidence, providing a supportive space for families navigating multiple languages and cultures.

title: Back carrying workshop
description:
In this workshop I will explain why we wear our babies on our backs as they grow. I will teach a method to train yourself and your child to be able to easily back carry. I will demonstrate several methods for getting your child on your back without anyone's help. We will then practice with dolls and/or with your own child and carriers. 

I will put out yoga mats for the little ones. I have one larger demo doll we can take turns with, and 2 smaller ones. Also pillows that I will weigh down that we can practice with. It is up to you if you want to bring your baby/toddler. They are very welcome and I will also bring a few toys for them. It would, in theory, be best to practice with them, but also, it's important that we, ourselves, practice first and get comfortable with the motions. And the patience of the little ones can run out which makes it hard for you to keep paying attention. So just think about what will be best for you.

We will sit for a bit and I will do some explaining at the beginning and then we will start to practice. I will have my collection of carriers there too that you can try, but you should also definitely bring the carriers you have. 

It will be fun and social!
newsletter_description: Certified babywearing consultant Zyanya will explain why we wear our babies on our backs as they grow. She will teach a method to train yourself and your child to be able to easily back carry. I will demonstrate several methods for getting your child on your back without anyone's help, and then practice with dolls and/or with your own child and carriers.

title: A Market for Mothers and their Little Ones
description: This market in Weesp is designed for all mothers (to be) and their little ones, offering a unique opportunity to meet and connect with various experts. Attendees can learn from a baby sleep coach, work coach, relationship therapist, pelvic floor physiotherapist (with diastasis check), postpartum expert/doula, and a pre/postnatal trainer. Entry to the event is free.
newsletter_description: The Tribe Weesp invites mothers (to be) and their little ones to a free market, where they can connect with various experts, including baby sleep coaches, physiotherapists, postpartum specialists, and pre/postnatal trainers.
location: Van Houten Industriepark 8, 1381 MZ Weesp, Netherlands

title: Adapted Sports & Games for Young Children
description: i-Sport Special organizes adapted sports and games for young children aged 2-5. These sessions focus on inclusive play and physical activity. The event is free, and registration is possible for one morning per week, either Monday or Wednesday morning.
newsletter_description: Join i-Sport Special for free adapted sports and games tailored for young children aged 2-5. These engaging sessions are designed for inclusive play and physical activity, happening weekly on Monday and Wednesday mornings.

title: FINDING YOURSELF AGAIN: Navigating Identity in Motherhood
description: Becoming a mother isn’t just about having a baby; it’s a profound identity shift known as matrescence. Many women feel like they don’t quite recognize themselves after becoming a mother. Join coach & counsellor Anca Zinculescu from A2Z Coaching for an intimate workshop exploring the emotional and psychological journey of becoming a mother. Through guided discussion and reflection, participants will gain language, insight, and practical tools to navigate this transformation with more self-compassion. This workshop is ideal for new mothers feeling a little lost, pregnant and preparing for motherhood, those navigating the gap between expectations and reality, or experiencing conflicting emotions.
newsletter_description: Maison Parentally invites mothers to a workshop, 'Finding Yourself Again: Navigating Identity in Motherhood.' Led by certified coach Anca Zinculescu, this intimate session explores the profound identity shift of matrescence, offering tools and insights for new and expectant mothers. Spots are limited, and registration is required.

title: Van Gogh Museum Parent & Baby Meetup
description: Join a specially-crafted meetup at the Van Gogh Museum for parents and their pre-walking babies (0-1 year old). Hosted by Amsterdam Parent Project, and Paola Bucciol (author of the ABCs of Amsterdam), this meetup provides an opportunity to connect with local parents and learn about Van Gogh's famous works like Sunflowers and Wheatfield with Crows. The 1 hour 30 minute meetup is designed to be flexible, allowing time for feeding or changing babies. After the gallery visit, there's an optional chat at the museum cafe. Please note that while the guided tour has a fee, participants are responsible for their own museum entrance ticket (via Museumkaart or purchased online).
newsletter_description: APP is hosting a special meetup for parents and their pre-walking babies (0-1 year old), in collaboration with Paola Bucciol. Connect with other parents while exploring Van Gogh's masterpieces, followed by a chance to meet and chat with fellow families at the museum cafe.

title: Museumstoomtram Hoorn-Medemblik Tulip Trip
description: Hop on the Museumstoomtram Hoorn-Medemblik for the ultimate 2026 tulip experience! This 'Living Museum' takes you on a journey with a 1920s steam engine through raw production fields and historic stations like Wognum and Twisk, where the best tulips can be found. The peak bloom season runs from April 15–25, 2026, with popular daily departures like the 10:40 AM ride.
newsletter_description: Take a magical 'time travel' trip on the Museumstoomtram Hoorn-Medemblik to experience the stunning 2026 tulip fields! This unique steam tram adventure, suitable for ages 4-12, offers rides through blooming landscapes and stops at historic stations daily during the peak bloom season from April 15–25. While tickets are €29.50 for adults and €21.00 for kids, entry is free with a Dutch Museum Card (Museumkaart).

title: Workshop: Mastering the Postpartum Period
description: This online workshop by Mamathrive, held in Dutch, is specifically designed for expectant parents to prepare for the postpartum period and the crucial 'fourth trimester.' It aims to provide a realistic understanding of what to expect after childbirth, covering essential themes such as physical and emotional recovery, sleep challenges, hormonal changes, nutrition, and the identity shift into parenthood. Participants will learn to create a personal postpartum plan, ensuring they approach the initial weeks after delivery with peace, clarity, and confidence. This preparation lays a strong foundation for a smooth recovery and a positive start to family life. The workshop includes a bonus 30-minute 1:1 follow-up call within four days to further refine your personal plan. It is open to individuals or couples and is limited to a small group of 5 couples to ensure personalized attention. The investment is €37 per couple.
newsletter_description: Mamathrive is hosting an online workshop in Dutch for expectant parents to prepare for the postpartum period. Learn how to create a personal postpartum plan covering recovery, sleep, and emotional well-being to ensure a smooth transition into parenthood. The workshop costs €37 per couple and includes a bonus 1:1 follow-up call.

title: moveNmeet Grand Opening Party & Parent-Child Sports Class Preview
description: Join moveNmeet for their Grand Opening Party! This celebratory event is designed for local parents, network partners, and anyone interested in a party and a mini-workout. Children are also very welcome. Attendees will have the opportunity to experience a preview of moveNmeet's parent & child sports class. The event takes place at Broedplaats SUP in Amsterdam West, offering a welcoming atmosphere for families.
newsletter_description: Join moveNmeet for a free Grand Opening Party, featuring a mini-workout and a preview of their parent-child sports class! This event welcomes local parents, network partners, and their children to celebrate and connect in Amsterdam West. Designed for families with young children.

title: Baby Massage Course
description: This 5-lesson course, offered by Mirjam Vos, teaches baby massage techniques, focusing on different body parts each week. It includes attention to haptonomic handling (picking up, carrying, putting down) and baby yoga exercises. The course also provides a valuable space for parents to connect and share experiences. Private sessions are available for babies who are restless or cry a lot, or for self-organized groups.
newsletter_description: Join Mirjam Vos for a comprehensive 5-lesson Baby Massage Course designed for infants 0-12 months. This course offers parents techniques to soothe their babies, aid digestion, and deepen bonding through touch, while also providing a space to connect with other parents. Check the website for course dates and registration details.

title: The Postpartum Table
description: Hello Doula and Arizen are hosting 'The Postpartum Table' at Papote Amsterdam, an evening dedicated to the importance of nutrition after birth. This event features a three-course postpartum meal, discussions about ingredients, and sharing cultural traditions around birth and recovery. Attendees will receive recipes and a useful pantry list to take home. Tickets are €60 per person or €100 for two, with only 20 spots available. Doors open at 18:00, and the event concludes at 20:30.
newsletter_description: Hello Doula and Arizen invite new mothers, mothers-to-be, partners, and their support network to 'The Postpartum Table' at Papote Amsterdam. This evening includes a three-course meal, discussions on postpartum nutrition, and sharing cultural traditions around birth and recovery. Participants will also receive recipes and a pantry list. Tickets are €60-€100, for adults interested in postpartum care and community.

title: Rabo Fat Tire Race
description: The Rabo Fat Tire Race, presented by Cycle Fun Productions, is a fun cycling and running event for children and families. The program includes balance bike races for 2-4 year olds, scooter races for 3-5 and 6-8 year olds, and fat tire bike races for various age groups from 3-6 years up to 11-12 years. There's also a 1km Family Run open to all ages. Participation is free. Registration and course reconnaissance open at 12:00. Coffee and snacks are available at Buurthuis Gein.
newsletter_description: Join Cycle Fun Productions for the free Rabo Fat Tire Race, a day filled with exciting cycling and running activities for children and families in Gein, Amsterdam! Kids from 2 to 12 years can participate in various balance bike, scooter, and fat tire races, while the 1km Family Run welcomes all ages for a fun-filled experience.

title: Meeting Mothers & Little Ones
description: This is a free meeting for mothers and their babies or young children, hosted by Maminfo. The April edition focuses on support and networking. It aims to create a safe space for mothers to discuss support during pregnancy and postpartum, asking for help without guilt, building social networks, and experiencing motherhood far from home. It is particularly suitable for pregnant women and mothers with babies or young children, especially those with a migration background seeking connection and recognition. Babies and young children are welcome. The meeting is hybrid and will be held in Dutch, Spanish, and English. Pre-registration is required due to limited places. The exact location in Amsterdam Zuidoost will be provided after registration.
newsletter_description: Maminfo invites mothers and their little ones (ages 0-4 years) to a free, multilingual (Dutch, Spanish, English) gathering in Amsterdam Zuidoost. This April edition focuses on support and networking, offering a safe space to discuss the challenges of motherhood and build community. It is especially suited for pregnant women and mothers with a migration background.

title: Tiny Grooves Dance Party
description: Tiny Grooves invites parents and young children to a vibrant dance party at Sissi's. Get ready to 'let it all out on the dance floor' and enjoy the new spring energy. Tickets are required for entry.
newsletter_description: Tiny Grooves is hosting a lively dance party for young children and their parents at Sissi's in Amsterdam East. Get ready to 'let it all out on the dance floor' and enjoy some vibrant spring energy. This event requires tickets and is ideal for ages 1-6 years.
location: Czaar Peterstraat 104, 1018 PW Amsterdam, The Netherlands

title: The Whole Neighborhood Bakes!
description: Join the third edition of 'The Whole Neighborhood Bakes!', a fun baking competition hosted by De Buurtkringloop. This event seeks passionate home bakers from the Indische Buurt to present their homemade cakes, with ingredients reimbursed up to €25. A professional jury will evaluate the creations. Registration is required by April 9th via email at events@debuurtkringloop.nl.
newsletter_description: De Buurtkringloop invites all ages to the third edition of 'The Whole Neighborhood Bakes!', a free community baking competition. Passionate home bakers from the Indische Buurt can present their homemade cakes to a professional jury, with ingredients reimbursed up to €25. Registration is required to participate.

title: The Girl Crying on a Stone
description: 't Zonnehuis Op Zijn Kop' (Zonnehuis Upside Down) is a youth theater series by Stichting Stadsherstel Cultureel in Amsterdam-Noord. This specific performance, 'The Girl Crying on a Stone' by Het Kleine Theater, is a cheerful, musical show full of old Dutch songs. It tells the story of a sad girl from a song who embarks on a journey to find a happier existence and unexpectedly discovers companionship.
organization: Stichting Stadsherstel Cultureel & Het Kleine Theater
newsletter_description: Join Het Kleine Theater for 'The Girl Crying on a Stone,' a cheerful and musical performance for ages 3+ years, part of the 'Zonnehuis Op Zijn Kop' series by Stichting Stadsherstel Cultureel. This delightful show, featuring old Dutch songs, tells the story of a sad girl seeking a happier existence and finding unexpected friendship at Zonnehuis Amsterdam. Tickets are required.

title: Tour the Farmer: The Origin
description: A free 'Tour the Farmer' event in Tuinen van West, focusing on 'The Origin' of food and combating food waste. The open day includes an activity where visitors can help feed piglets with residual food streams. There will also be a demonstration by Dirk's Smoke Machine, providing warm meat sandwiches. This event encourages young and old to participate in sustainable practices.
newsletter_description: Join the Entrepreneurs' Association Gardens of West for a free 'Tour the Farmer' event, 'De Herkomst' (The Origin), open to all ages. Help feed adorable piglets with residual food streams and learn about combating food waste. This event encourages families to engage with sustainable practices and understand the origin of their food.

title: Princess on the Pea
description: Prince Alexander Ludovik Stoffel van Stavernijen must marry a real princess with a delicate and sensitive skin. He travels the world with his horse Hendrik to find her, but without success. Then, on a stormy night, she suddenly stands before his gate – a real princess! Now he must find out if she truly has delicate and sensitive skin. Don't miss this children's theater performance.
newsletter_description: Join Het Kleine Theater for a delightful performance of 'Princess on the Pea' at Hal West Amsterdam. Prince Alexander is on a quest to find a real princess with sensitive skin, and on a stormy night, one finally appears! This engaging children's theater experience is suitable for ages 3+ and tickets are €7,50.
location: Louis Couperusstraat 150, 1064 EX Amsterdam, The Netherlands

title: Bostheater Opening Party
description: Join Hét openluchttheater van het Amsterdamse Bos for their free opening party to celebrate the start of the new season. Get a sneak peek of what's to come this season at the Bostheater. This event is fun for all ages, and families, friends, and children are encouraged to attend.
organization: Hét openluchttheater van het Amsterdamse Bos
newsletter_description: Join Hét openluchttheater van het Amsterdamse Bos for their free opening party to celebrate the start of the new season! This event, perfect for all ages, offers a sneak peek of what's to come at the Bostheater this year.

title: Musical Journey Across the Atlantic
description: Duo Joana Almeida (vocals) and Pedro Ivo (guitar) invite you on a musical journey across the Atlantic Ocean. This event is specially designed for children aged 2 and up and their parents/caregivers, featuring Brazilian classics and songs adapted for the occasion. It promises a journey full of color, fun, and emotion! After the concert, attendees will have the opportunity to discover and try out instruments from Brazil.
newsletter_description: Teatro Munganga invites families with children aged 2 and up to a 'Musical Journey Across the Atlantic' with Duo Joana Almeida and Pedro Ivo. This vibrant concert features Brazilian classics, promising a journey full of color, fun, and emotion, followed by an opportunity for kids and parents to try out instruments from Brazil. Tickets are required for this engaging musical event.
location: Schinkelhavenstraat 27 HS, 1075 VS Amsterdam, The Netherlands

title: May Holiday Kids Activities at De Natuurkamer
description: Join De Natuurkamer for three days of special May Holiday kids' activities, including baking bread, pony rides, theater performances, and various workshops. These activities are part of a broader May Holiday program at Sloterpark and cost €2 per activity or €5 for a passe-partout, with free access for Stadspas holders. Some workshops may require reservation.
newsletter_description: De Natuurkamer invites children aged 2-10 years to three days of fun May Holiday activities including baking bread, pony rides, theater, and workshops. This event, part of their larger holiday program, costs €2 per activity (or €5 for a passe-partout) and is free with a Stadspas.

title: Mingle for Business Mamas
description: This unhosted Mama Mingle, created by MamaSocial Amsterdam, is specifically for Business Mamas: entrepreneurial, freelance, and career-focused mothers seeking connection and real conversation. It offers a relaxed, come-as-you-are vibe in a cosy, baby- and laptop-friendly café spot, perfect for sharing ideas, venting, or simply enjoying the company of like-minded women. Little ones are always welcome, whether solo or as mini CEO-in-training. There are no pitches or panels, just genuine connection without calendar overload. This ticket secures your seat only; food and drinks are paid individually at the venue.
newsletter_description: The Business Mamas Mingle by MamaSocial offers entrepreneurial and career-focused mothers a relaxed, unhosted meetup to connect, share insights, and enjoy real conversation. Held in baby-friendly cafes, it's a supportive space where little ones (0-4 years) are always welcome, allowing moms to balance work and motherhood.

title: May Holiday Sports Festival
description: Join Monkey Moves Amsterdam for a fun-filled sports festival during the May holiday. This parent-child event offers an active morning of gym activities for toddlers and preschoolers, designed for children aged 1.5 to 6 years. Prepare to sweat and have a great time! Registration is required as spots are limited.
newsletter_description: Monkey Moves Amsterdam invites children aged 1.5-6 years and their parents to a lively May Holiday Sports Festival at Het Marnix. Enjoy an active morning filled with toddler and preschooler gym activities, promising lots of fun and movement. Registration is required to secure your spot.

title: Sensory-friendly Family Evening - Rijksmuseum
description: The Rijksmuseum offers special sensory-friendly family evenings designed for families and children of all ages who require a low-stimulus environment. This includes individuals with autism, ADHD, tinnitus, brain injury, or visual impairments. During these evenings, the museum opens after regular closing hours (6-9 PM) with fewer visitors, providing a calm atmosphere. Guests can find quiet spaces, visit the museum shop and café, and use free audio tours. Private guided tours are also available for an additional fee of €30 per group (max 6 people). Only the 17th and 19th-century sections of the museum are open during these events.
newsletter_description: The Rijksmuseum hosts special sensory-friendly family evenings, offering a calm and accessible environment for children and families of all ages who need a low-stimulus experience. Enjoy a quieter visit to the 17th and 19th-century sections of the museum after regular closing hours, with fewer crowds and dedicated quiet spaces.

title: Starting Solids: Complete Roadmap Workshop
description: Join Petit Gourmet Academy for an informative and supportive workshop on starting solids. This session provides practical guidance and evidence-based insights to help parents confidently navigate this milestone. Topics covered include when and how to start, understanding baby's development for safe texture introduction, differentiating gagging from choking, introducing allergens, balanced meals, and realistic daily meal ideas.
newsletter_description: Learn to confidently navigate the exciting milestone of starting solids for your baby with this informative workshop by Mélody from Petit Gourmet Academy. Designed for parents of babies 4-12 months old, it covers everything from readiness signs and food introduction to managing allergens and balanced meal ideas.

title: Puppet Theatre Koos Kneus: The Oddball
description:
Poppentheater Koos Kneus is a cozy puppet theatre in Amsterdam-Oost.

When? Children’s performances take place every Sunday. There are shows aimed at toddlers aged 2 to 4 years and shows for children aged 4 to 8 years. Please check our calendar for show times.

What? The performances last 30 to 45 minutes.

Face paint. After and before the shows, a professional face painter will turn all children into beautiful princesses, tigers, pirates or butterflies. There are lots of dress-up sets to wear too.

Food/drink. There’s free coffee, tea, lemonade and water. Also, help yourself to our signature speculaasjes!

In Dutch? All performances are conducted in Dutch, but the toddler shows especially are not language-heavy and easy to follow.

Birthday party If you celebrate your son or daughter’s birthday party here, the party will be cosy among the other visitors in the theatre. Moreover, your birthday boy or girl will be completely put in the limelight! He/she gets to come forward, is sung to and gets to pick out a present from the Koos Kneus gift selection box!

You can possibly bring your own cake, cupcakes or pie, but it is not strictly necessary as there is always a tumbler on the big table with more than enough biscuits for everyone! More snacks than a simple cake or pie are not allowed. Perhaps you can bring your own cute disposable plates? There are sometimes several children’s parties in the theatre at the same time. So a party with everything on it!

Everything will then be over at 12.15pm (the 11am performance) or 3pm (the 1pm performance). Agree with the parents of the friends that they can come and collect their children at 12.15 and 15.00 at our door respectively.

Where? Our theatre is located at Iepenplein 40 in Amsterdam-Oost (near metro station Wibautstraat and OLVG-Oost hospital). Find directions here.
newsletter_description: Poppentheater Koos Kneus offers captivating puppet shows designed for young children aged 2-8 years. This mobile theatre brings enchanting storytelling through puppets to various locations, creating magical experiences for families. In this month's performance, a clumsy witch accidentally conjures up a strange bird full of tricks. He even makes life miserable for the sweet fairy...

title: Puppet shows on beach IJburg
description: Poppenkast IJburg offers a series of engaging, family-friendly puppet shows perfect for children (typically ages 3 to 8) and their families. A face painter is also present at the shows. Shows are in Dutch but puppetry is universal and visually interesting for children. Shows are 30-45 minutes long. Children can play on the sandy beach.
newsletter_description: Poppenkast IJburg presents engaging puppet shows on IJburg beach, offering a delightful experience for children aged 3 to 8 and their families. In addition to the visually captivating shows, a face painter will be present, and kids can enjoy playing on the sandy beach.
location: VriJland Pampuslaan 503, 1087 BE Amsterdam, The Netherlands

title: Liberation Day Celebration in De Banne
description: Join VoorUit De Banne for a vibrant Liberation Day celebration in De Banne, Amsterdam. This community event, offering music, food, activities, and opportunities for connection, welcomes all ages to reflect on and celebrate freedom. The day begins at 11:00 with a Musical Freedom Parade from Lichterstraat, leading to various activities until 17:00 across multiple locations like OBA Banne, Huis van de Wijk De Banne, 't Kofschip, De Ark, Speeltuin De Zeeslag, and De Rietwijker.
newsletter_description: VoorUit De Banne invites families to a vibrant Liberation Day celebration in De Banne, Amsterdam. This free event features a musical parade, live music, food, drinks, and various activities for all ages across multiple community centers and playgrounds in the neighborhood. Reflect on and celebrate freedom with your community.

title: Money Webinar
description: A free monthly webinar dedicated to helping future and current parents understand the financial support available from the Dutch government. Hosted by Mama Maria Coach, together with an independent financial planner, the session walks participants through various subsidies and answers questions, aiming to make parents feel informed, confident, and supported. Dates and times for upcoming sessions are shared via the Mama Maria Coach Instagram (@mamamariacoach) and a dedicated WhatsApp group.
newsletter_description: Join Mamamaria for a free monthly online webinar designed to help future and current parents navigate the financial support available from the Dutch government. This session, co-hosted with an independent financial planner, clarifies subsidies and answers your questions, empowering you with confidence and support. The date of the upcoming session is decided by poll in the WhatsApp group.

title: Puppet Show Street at Het Vrije Westen
description: Experience the lively 'Puppet Show Street' at Het Vrije Westen festival, featuring a diverse array of traditional and contemporary puppet shows. Audiences can enjoy classic Jan Klaassen and Katrijn tales presented by Poppenkast op de Dam (performing since 1893), modern interpretations by Erik van Ophem, and performances by Els van den Burg and Poppentheater de Fighter. These highly interactive shows are designed for all ages, encouraging audience participation to help the characters through their adventures. This event, celebrating freedom and community spirit, is typically held during the annual Liberation Festival.
newsletter_description: Join Het Vrije Westen for their 'Puppet Show Street', a delightful collection of traditional and contemporary puppet shows perfect for children aged 0-12 years. Featuring interactive performances by classic characters like Jan Klaassen, this free event brings together various puppet theaters for a fun-filled afternoon in Westerpark.
location: Westerpark, Haarlemerweg 8, 1014 LS Amsterdam, The Netherlands

title: KinderBevrijdingsfestival
description: Celebrate Liberation Day with the whole family at the Children's Liberation Festival. Jeugdtheaterhuis Amsterdam presents 'De Kinderen van de Hollandse Schouwburg' (The Children of the Hollandse Schouwburg, 8+), an interactive musical performance about Jewish children in World War II. Achref Adhadhi & Kristján Martinsson perform 'Thuis Best' (Home Best, 2+), a poetic mime performance about feeling at home. The festival also features dance workshops, theater and craft activities, and stories from older Amsterdammers about their war experiences. The festival concludes with a funky performance by Naga Kirana, with music by DJ Misja Impossible throughout the day.
newsletter_description: Join 4 en 5 mei Amsterdam for the KinderBevrijdingsfestival, a Children's Liberation Festival in Vondelpark. Enjoy interactive musical and mime performances, dance workshops, craft activities, and inspiring stories from older Amsterdammers. With activities for ages 2+ years, it's a day of celebration, learning, and fun for the whole family.

title: Zeeslag Kids Liberation Festival
description: Come to our Liberation Festival at Speeltuin Zeeslag on May 5th! Children can enjoy crafting and participate in various fun activities. There's a bouncy castle, smoothie making, face painting, and dancing to cheerful music from the DJ. Additionally, there's a bar with healthy snacks and fruity drinks.
newsletter_description: Zeeslag Kids Liberation Festival invites children to celebrate Liberation Day with a free festival at Speeltuin Zeeslag. Kids can enjoy a bouncy castle, crafts, face painting, smoothie making, and dancing to music. Perfect for children aged 1-12 years.

title: Art Club: Fishy Craft Session
description: Dive into creativity at Art Club! Join Robbeburg International Playgroup for a fun, fishy-themed craft session where little artists can make a splash with their imagination. Spaces are limited, and tickets are required.
newsletter_description: Join Robbeburg International Playgroup for a fun, fishy-themed craft session at their Art Club! Little artists can make a splash with their imagination and create unique aquatic masterpieces. Tickets are required for this creative event, designed for children aged 2-5 years.

title: Suzuki Music for Babies & Toddlers – Spring Programme
description: Join your child in discovering the wonders of music, movement, storytelling and community in these Suzuki Music classes. Designed for children aged 0-3 and their parent or caregiver, the program encourages musical, motor, cognitive, and social skills through singing, dancing, and playing simple instruments. Based on Dr. Shinichi Suzuki’s belief that “Every child can learn,” the method fosters curiosity, confidence, and a lifelong love of learning. Classes are held weekly on Thursdays and are taught in English and Dutch, welcoming all languages. Participants can sign up for the full 6-class Spring 2026 programme for €111 or try a single class for €7.50.
newsletter_description: Discover the joy of music with your little one at the Suzuki Music for Babies & Toddlers Spring Programme. These weekly classes, designed for children aged 0-3 years and their caregivers, involve singing, dancing, and playing instruments to foster musical, motor, and social skills. Classes are offered at €111 for the full 6-week program or €7.50 for a trial class.

title: Amazing Giveaway for Amsterdam Mamas
description: An amazing giveaway for Amsterdam moms, featuring prizes from local businesses. One lucky winner will receive a mini session with Sim Sawyers Photography, a color analysis with Clothes and Confidence, a 30-minute massage with Sy Relax Massage, and a quilted utility bag from Mirai Looms. To enter, follow all four accounts, like the Instagram post, and tag a mom who would love this. Bonus entries are available for saving, sharing to stories, and reposting the post. The winner will be announced on May 7th.
organization: Sim Sawyers Photography, Clothes and Confidence, Sy Relax Massage, Mirai Looms
newsletter_description: 4 local mom-run businesses are hosting an amazing giveaway 🎁 One lucky winner will receive a mini session with Sim Sawyers Photography, a color analysis with Clothes and Confidence, a 30-minute massage with Sy Relax Massage, and a quilted utility bag from Mirai Looms.

title: Stronger Together: Mental Health Community Walk (Amsterdam)
description: Join Relocation Counseling for a free community walk in Vondelpark, Amsterdam, as part of European Mental Health Week. This event aims to connect people living abroad or building a new life in a different country, and those who support them. It's an opportunity to create space for connection, acknowledge the emotional impact of living abroad, move away from isolation towards community, and reflect on collective mental health support. This is a simple, intentional walk to be together, not a networking event or therapy session. Donations are accepted and will be given to The Regenboog Groep.
newsletter_description: Relocation Counseling & Consulting invites you to a free 'Stronger Together' Mental Health Community Walk in Amsterdam's Vondelpark. This event provides a space for connection and shared experience for those living abroad, fostering community and collective mental health support. Children under 16 are welcome with a parent or legal guardian.

title: Mother's Day Giveaway by Growing Happy Bellies
description: Growing Happy Bellies is hosting a Mother's Day giveaway for mamas in Amsterdam. One winner will receive a 'Starter Belly tier' of ready-to-heat toddler meals. Participants need to like, follow, share the post to their story, and optionally tag a deserving mama to enter. The winner will be announced on May 8th, with delivery on May 9th.
newsletter_description: Growing Happy Bellies is celebrating Mother's Day with a special giveaway for mothers in Amsterdam. One lucky mom will win a 'Starter Belly tier' of delicious, ready-to-heat meals for their toddlers. It's a wonderful opportunity for moms with toddlers (ages 1-3 years) to get a break from meal planning. Entry is free through Instagram.

title: Mother's Day Drink, Pastry & Paint in Amsterdam
description: Join Anook Bakehouse for a special Mother's Day Paint & Sip workshop, a cozy 2h30 session to unleash your creativity in a warm, welcoming atmosphere. Designed for everyone, with or without painting experience, you'll be guided step-by-step to complete your own 'Tulip flowers' artwork to take home. The workshop is available in English and French and includes all art supplies, a drink, and a pastry. It's the perfect way for mothers and friends to relax, connect, and express themselves creatively. Spots are limited, so booking is essential.
newsletter_description: Anook Bakehouse invites mothers and friends to a special Mother's Day Paint & Sip workshop, where you can relax and unleash your creativity. This guided session includes all art supplies to paint 'Tulip flowers,' a drink, and pastry, making it a perfect adult outing to create a personal masterpiece. Tickets are required for this special event.
location: Van Woustraat 79, 1073 LV Amsterdam, The Netherlands

title: Mingle x Papote: the parent social
description:
Mingle Community x Papote present: 
A gezellige vrijmibo (look this up if you don't know it!) for international parents & parents to be, where we combine Dutch practice and socialising. 

Friday, May 8
5.45PM – 7.15PM (ish, feel free to leave whenever you like)
📍 Papote Café – Vasco Da Gamastraat 65, De Baarsjes, Amsterdam

What is this event?
An easy, feel-good Friday afternoon where you can:
- meet like-minded people
- practice a bit of Dutch (in a super relaxed way)
- and just enjoy a carefree moment

No pressure & no awkward networking: just gezellige vibes, nice conversations, and a glass of something you like 🥂

And the best part? Your kids can play in the play area while you unwind.

Why this event?
Papote & Mingle are joining forces to bring international parents together 🤍

We’re actually neighbours (how cute is that?!) — Papote Café is on one corner of the street, and Mingle Community / Tupkertaaltraining is on the other. 

By combining our spaces and communities, we’re creating a place where you can:
✨ connect with other parents
✨ feel at home in Amsterdam
✨ pick up some Dutch along the way

You're welcome to order more food/drinks at the cafe if you'd like to have dinner too! <3
newsletter_description: Mingle Community x Papote present a gezellige vrijmibo (look this up if you don't know it!) for international parents & parents to be, where we combine Dutch practice and socializing.

title: Mother's Day Neighbourhood Gift Swap
description: This Mother’s Day, SwapShare invites the community to a neighbourhood gift swap at Move Collective, located at Overtoom 87 in Amsterdam. Skip the shopping rush and swap gifts for Mother's Day. The event offers free entrance and runs from 13:00 to 16:00, providing a sustainable way to find unique gifts.
newsletter_description: SwapShare is hosting a free Mother's Day neighbourhood gift swap at Move Collective in Oud-West. This event encourages families to find unique and sustainable gifts by swapping items, offering a mindful alternative to traditional shopping.

title: Mother's Day Mini-Retreat
description: A Mother's Day Mini-Retreat dedicated to the joy and well-being of mothers. This 3.5-hour event invites mothers to step out of the 'mental load' and into a space designed for nourishment, expression, and connection. Held at the beautiful Damoves Studio, the afternoon includes a Singing Mamas Circle led by Danielle Bensky, where participants sing together in harmony to soothe themselves. It also features a Sharing Circle, time for tea, treats, and mingling, and a Fluentbody Session led by Merav Zvulun, a somatic movement practice for release and recharge. The event concludes with a gentle closure. All mothers are welcome, and non-crawling babies are also welcome. The investment is €75, which includes tea and light snacks. Space is limited to 15 women to ensure an intimate circle.
newsletter_description: This Mother's Day, join Singing Mamas and Fluentbody for a Mini-Retreat dedicated to maternal well-being. Enjoy an afternoon of song, somatic movement, and community connection, designed for mothers (non-crawling babies are welcome, too!). Space is limited to 15 women for an intimate experience.

title: Bao Brunch - Mother's Day Edition
description: Hotel Jakarta Amsterdam organizes a Mother's Day edition of the Bao Brunch at Skybar Malabar. Enjoy a unique 3-course brunch experience with a beautiful view over Amsterdam, featuring delicious Bao Buns and surprising snacks, combined with matching cocktails. Limited spots are available. Price: €54,- per person.
newsletter_description: Celebrate Mother's Day with a special Bao Brunch at Hotel Jakarta Amsterdam's Skybar Malabar. Indulge in a unique 3-course menu of delicious Bao Buns and snacks, complemented by cocktails, all while enjoying stunning views of Amsterdam.

title: Mother's Day Chair Massages
description: Papote offers a special Mother's Day surprise for mothers: complimentary chair massages given by a hired masseuse. The massages will be available between 3:30 PM and 5:30 PM and are exclusively for mothers.
newsletter_description: Papote is offering a special Mother's Day treat for all mothers: complimentary chair massages. Come and enjoy a relaxing massage provided by a professional masseuse, available between 3:30 PM and 5:30 PM. This event is free and exclusively for mothers.

title: Musical reading workshop 'We hebben er een geitje bij'
description: Two spots have just become available for this musical reading workshop, based on the picture book 'We hebben er een geitje bij', which was named Picture Book of the Year in 2016. The workshop is hosted by De Muziekzolder and is suitable for children aged 2 to 7 years. Tickets are required.
newsletter_description: De Muziekzolder invites children aged 2-7 years to a lively musical reading workshop! Based on the acclaimed picture book 'We hebben er een geitje bij' (Picture Book of the Year 2016), this interactive session promises engaging storytelling and musical fun.

title: Birth Team Clarity: Who Supports You & When It Matters Most
description: This online masterclass, hosted by Birth Nest | Doula, is designed for parents to understand the complexities of the Dutch maternity system. It clarifies the roles of various professionals like midwives, obstetricians, kraamzorg, lactation consultants, and doulas during pregnancy, birth, and postpartum care. Attendees will learn about the complete birth timeline for various situations, what happens during a transfer of care, and gain practical tools to craft their birth plan with confidence, fostering clarity and informed decision-making. The session also addresses myths, misconceptions, and the emotional landscape of pregnancy and birth, offering insights into navigating fears and past experiences.
newsletter_description: Join Birth Nest | Doula for an online masterclass, 'Birth Team Clarity', designed for expecting and new parents in the Netherlands. This session demystifies the Dutch maternity system, explaining the roles of midwives, doulas, kraamzorg, and other professionals during pregnancy, birth, and postpartum. Gain confidence and clarity to build your ideal birth team and make informed decisions.

title: Mother's Day Celebration & Creative Kids Workshop
description: WOPL! hosts a special Mother's Day-themed event on May 12th, offering free entry for adults and a creative workshop for children. The event runs from 10:00 to 16:00, providing a full day of play and learning activities at their World of Play & Learning center.
newsletter_description: Join WOPL! for a special Mother's Day celebration on May 12th, featuring free entry for adults and an engaging creative workshop for kids (2-8 years). It's a fantastic opportunity for families to enjoy a day of play, learning, and connection at WOPL!'s World of Play & Learning.

title: Mama Mingle - New Mamas (Pregnant Mamas also welcome!)
description: The MamaSocial 'New Mamas Mingle' is a casual, unhosted meetup designed for pregnant mamas and new mamas seeking connection and conversation. It offers a relaxed, come-as-you-are vibe at a handpicked, baby and toddler-friendly cafe in Amsterdam. There's no set agenda, schedule, or host, providing a low-pressure environment to chat, laugh, and feel more connected during the new mama months. While the meetup itself is free to attend, individual food and drink consumption at the venue will be charged to each participant. These mingles are ad-hoc and may be cancelled if not enough mamas sign up, so check the website for specific dates and details.
newsletter_description: Need a little time for yourself, a warm drink in hand, and some grown-up conversation? New Mamas Mingle is a casual, unhosted meetup where you can show up as you are - messy bun, pram in tow, or solo if it’s a daycare day. It's designed for pregnant mamas and new mamas seeking connection and conversation.

title: Become a Support Family with Buurtgezinnen
description: Buurtgezinnen (Neighborhood Families) is an initiative that connects families in need of support with local volunteer 'Support Families' (Steungezinnen). Support families offer practical help and a listening ear, such as having a child join them for an afternoon, offering regular weekend stays, or doing fun activities with children. The time commitment is flexible, often involving half a day weekly or bi-weekly. This voluntary work is for individuals or families with experience with children, requires a Certificate of Good Conduct (VOG), and is supported by a local Buurtgezinnen coordinator. The program operates in over 145 municipalities across the Netherlands, aiming to prevent problems from escalating and ensuring children can stay in their own homes.
newsletter_description: Buurtgezinnen connects families needing extra support with local 'support families.' This free volunteering opportunity allows you to offer or receive practical help (like meal prep and babysitting) and a listening ear, with flexible time commitments. Thanks to APP community member Markus for the tip!

title: Postpartum Exchange
description:
Join APP for our monthly networking circle centered on the local experts providing postpartum support in Amsterdam. The goal is to connect, share experiences, and swap ideas with others in the field. Whether you’re a postpartum professional or just passionate about postpartum care, come hang out and grow your network in a friendly, supportive space.

 Each circle is limited to 5 professionals to keep the table intimate and conversation flowing. The first person to arrive gets a coffee on APP ❤️

 If this month's session is full, join the next one!

 Please note: This ticket holds your spot in the Exchange; food & drink must be purchased separately. (A great opportunity to support the local play café, run by toddler mom Sophia!)
newsletter_description: Join APP's new monthly Postpartum Exchange, an intimate networking circle for professionals passionate about postpartum support in Amsterdam. Connect, share insights, and expand your network in a supportive setting. First person to arrive gets a coffee on me ❤️

title: Workshop: The Emotional Reality of Parenting Abroad
description:
Raising children abroad can look exciting from the outside.But many parents quietly carry loneliness, jealousy, guilt, and doubt. This workshop creates space for what is often left unspoken.

We will explore the emotional reality of parenting abroad — the tension between the life you chose and how it sometimes feels.You will be invited to recognize your own experience, put words to it, and see that you are not alone in it.

This is not about fixing your situation.It is about understanding what is happening inside you — and finding a way to relate to it. A calm, honest space to reconnect with yourself as a parent living between cultures. 

Purpose:
To create a space where parents can recognize, name, and normalize the complex emotions of parenting abroad.

To learn to understand what happens when emotions feel overwhelming. Gain insights and strategies to deal with overwhelming emotions in a safe and easy way. 

Agenda:
Welcome & Introduction (10-15 min)
The emotions as a parent abroad (30 min)
What happens when emotions overwhelm (20 min.)
How to deal with overwhelm (20-30 min)
Wrap up (10-15 min) 
Total: 90-120 min 

Join this workshop to explore the emotional reality of parenting abroad — honestly, without pressure to have it all figured out.

Save your spot - spaces are limited.
newsletter_description: Join Michaela from Family Mentoring Amsterdam for a supportive workshop designed for parents living abroad. This session offers a safe space to explore and process the unique emotional challenges of international parenting, helping you connect with and release feelings like loneliness, jealousy, or frustration.

title: Youth Theater Performance My Colors
description: My Colors is a colorful, physical, and imaginative solo performance by Mime Wave, presented by DAT!school. Through puppetry, animations, music, illusions, and humor, it creates a visual world where fantasy and reality blend. The performance delves into the early years of a child's life, exploring how these foundational moments shape their identity. The story follows Mother Hare and her little Hare, a puppet that changes colors with each adventure, symbolizing that everyone has their own unique colors. This visual theater, puppetry, and animation blend aims to provide a special and poetic experience for children aged 2 and up.
newsletter_description: DAT!school presents 'My Colors,' a vibrant and imaginative solo theater performance by Mime Wave, perfect for children aged 2 and up. This visually rich show combines puppetry, animation, music, and humor to explore themes of identity and early childhood experiences. Tickets are required for this engaging theatrical journey.

title: Burnout Summit 2026: Self-Compassion in the Age of Burnout
description: This is a 2-day global online summit focused on 'Self-Compassion in the Age of Burnout'. It is designed for professionals, leaders, therapists, and those who support them, aiming to help them understand burnout deeply and recover in a way that actually lasts. The summit explores burnout from multiple angles including Neurodivergence, Women’s health & midlife, Nature & the body, Relationships & belonging, and Workplace & leadership. It features deep, thoughtful panel discussions, experiential workshops, honest conversations about pressure, identity, and burnout, and practical tools that attendees can apply in their lives and work. The event is free to attend live.
newsletter_description: The free 2-day online Burnout Summit offers a deep dive into self-compassion for professionals and leaders grappling with burnout. Hosted by Burnout Summit, this event provides practical tools and discussions from global experts on various facets of burnout, helping attendees understand and recover effectively. This event is not suitable for children.

title: Baby Pilates at Nippon Do
description: Baby Pilates classes for mothers focusing on postpartum recovery, including exercises for the pelvic floor, abdominal muscles, stabilization, and strength. Mothers can train together with their babies. Classes are guided by an exercise therapist Mensendieck and pilates instructor Hanne Blackstone. Partners are welcome to join. A free trial lesson can be booked.
newsletter_description: Join Nippon Do for Baby Pilates, a class designed for mothers focusing on postpartum recovery with exercises for the pelvic floor, abdominal muscles, stabilization, and strength. Mothers train together with their babies, guided by an exercise therapist and pilates instructor. Partners are welcome, and you can book a free trial lesson!

title: Play, Share, Exchange, and Recycle Event
description: Motherhood.amsterdam is hosting a 'Play, Share, Exchange, and Recycle' event. It's an opportunity for families to exchange children's items, perfect for decluttering after festivities like King's Day. The event takes place over two days at Treehouse NDSM in Amsterdam.
newsletter_description: The 'Play, Share, Exchange, and Recycle' event is back -- where families can swap children's clothes, toys, and other treasures. This two-day event at Treehouse NDSM is a great way to find new items and declutter for children aged 0-8 years.
location: T.T. Neveritaweg 55, 1033 WB Amsterdam, The Netherlands

title: Sheep Shearing Festival
description: Join Kinderboerderij de Pijp for a cozy Sheep Shearing Festival on Saturday, May 30th, from 13:00 to 16:00! Experience a live sheep shearing demonstration with expert explanation, and learn about wool spinning. Children and families can enjoy creative activities like tile decorating, coloring pages, and crafting sheep with real wool. There will also be geocaching on the farm, a chance to 'milk' your own lemonade from an art cow, face painting, petting guinea pigs, and a bouncy castle. Discover a special stall offering new clothes made from old ones. It's a fun neighborhood party for everyone!
newsletter_description: Kinderboerderij de Pijp invites families to their free Sheep Shearing Festival, a fun neighborhood party for all ages! Witness live sheep shearing and wool spinning demonstrations, enjoy creative workshops like tile decorating and wool crafting, and let the kids have fun with face painting, a bouncy castle, and petting guinea pigs.
location: Lizzy Ansinghstraat 82, 1072 RD Amsterdam, The Netherlands

title: School Choice in Amsterdam workshop
description: This interactive workshop, hosted by Kaleidoscope Consulting at Monti's World, is designed to help families of young children (ages 0-4) navigate the often overwhelming process of choosing a primary school in Amsterdam. Participants will gain a clearer understanding of how the system works, empowering them to make informed school choices. The workshop will cover topics such as deciding between international, public/state (Dutch), and bilingual schools, defining educational philosophies (Montessori, Dalton, Vrije etc.), explaining the Amsterdam school lottery system, assessing family values and needs, and tips for school tours. The session is presented by Isabel Cruz, an experienced educational consultant from Kaleidoscope Consulting. This is a paid workshop priced at €45 per family ticket.
newsletter_description: Navigating school choices in Amsterdam can be complex, but Isabel at Kaleidoscope Consulting offers an interactive workshop to help families understand the system. It covers various topics like international vs. Dutch schools, educational philosophies, and the Amsterdam school lottery system, empowering you to make informed decisions for your child's primary education.

title: Neighborhood Market & Chess Tournament
description: A vibrant community event featuring a neighborhood market, a bouncy castle for children, live music, and a chess tournament. Families can enjoy a variety of activities throughout the day. Stalls are available for rent for €7.50, and participants are invited to join the chess tournament by mailing Tom at tom_velpen@hotmail.com.
newsletter_description: Join a vibrant Neighborhood Market & Chess Tournament, a community event offering activities for all ages. Enjoy a bouncy castle for children, live music, a flea market, and participate in a chess tournament. Stalls are available for rent for €7.50, and the event is perfect for families looking for a fun day out.
location: Nicolaas Beetsplantsoen, 1054 KV Amsterdam, The Netherlands

title: AIC Kids & Parents: The Social Playdate @ Moxy
description: An exciting social playdate hosted by AIC for kids aged 2-10 and their parents at the Moxy Amsterdam Houthavens. The event features activities like games (e.g., Jenga), entertainment (e.g., TV/cartoons), and snacks (popcorn machine). It's a great opportunity for families to connect and socialize in a fun environment. Free parking is available for all guests at the hotel.
newsletter_description: Join AIC Kids & Parents for a fun social playdate at Moxy Amsterdam Houthavens, designed for children aged 2-10 and their families. Enjoy engaging activities like games and entertainment, complete with snacks and free parking for a relaxed gathering. It's a perfect opportunity for families to connect and build community.

title: Children's Clothing Market at Speeltuin de Waag
description: A sustainable and cozy children's clothing market where families can find new outfits for their kids and sell clothes that have become too small. Enjoy a fun day with a bouncy castle and drinks. Registration for sellers opens on May 11th, and spots are limited.
newsletter_description: The Lastage Markt is hosting a sustainable children's clothing market at Speeltuin de Waag. Families can find new outfits for kids, sell outgrown clothes, and enjoy a lively day with a bouncy castle and drinks. This community event is suitable for children aged 0-10 years.

title: Chamber Music in the Royal Hall: The Grand Animal Carnival
description:
The Grand Animal Carnival is the traditional season-ending event for Kamermuziek in de Koningszaal (KaMuKoZa). It features the powerful score of Saint-Saëns' 'Carnival of the Animals'. Inspired by last year's success, the event will again include attractive accompanying music by pianist and composer Henry Kelder, based on Armando's 'Dierenpraat', featuring animals not originally set to music by Saint-Saëns, such as a bear, mouse, and crane. This musical event is the closing of KaMuKoZa's fourth season.

KaMuKoZa is part of ARTIS. These are the ticket prices:

General admission: € 17,50
ARTIS member: € 10
Student: € 12,50
Children: Free
newsletter_description: Kamermuziek in de Koningszaal (KaMuKoZa), part of ARTIS, invites families to their traditional season-ending Grand Animal Carnival, featuring Saint-Saëns' 'Carnival of the Animals'. The event also includes enchanting new music by composer Henry Kelder, bringing to life animals not in the original score.

title: 20 Years Netherlands Youth Orchestra
description: A grand anniversary concert celebrating 20 years of Leerorkest Nederland (Netherlands Youth Orchestra). This festive program brings together children from various schools and municipalities across the Netherlands, performing alongside the prestigious Royal Concertgebouw Orchestra. The event aims to provide an accessible musical experience for tens of thousands of children, fostering their growth through music and ensemble playing. A new arrangement of Vivaldi's 'The Four Seasons' by Bob Zimmerman will be a highlight. It promises to be an unforgettable afternoon for families and friends.
newsletter_description: Celebrate 20 years of Leerorkest Nederland with a grand anniversary concert at The Concertgebouw! Children from youth orchestras across the Netherlands will perform alongside the Royal Concertgebouw Orchestra in a festive (free!) program, featuring a new arrangement of Vivaldi's 'The Four Seasons'. This inspiring musical event, perfect for families and music lovers of all ages, requires tickets for entry.
